audit_status.source_base: Greek Revelation text tradition with Ezekiel/Isaiah throne imagery; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for open door, throne, living creatures, holy hymn, creator worship.

verses.restored: After these things I saw, and look, a door opened in heaven, and the first voice I heard like trumpet speaking with me said: Come up here, and I will show you what must happen after these things.
Immediately I came to be in Spirit, and look, a throne stood in heaven, and upon the throne One sitting.
And the One sitting was like jasper stone and carnelian in appearance, and a rainbow around the throne like emerald in appearance.
Around the throne were twenty-four thrones, and upon the thrones twenty-four elders sitting, clothed in white garments, with golden crowns upon their heads.
From the throne come flashes, voices, and thunders; and seven lamps of fire burning before the throne, which are the seven Spirits of the Divine.
Before the throne, as sea of glass like crystal; and in midst of throne and around throne, four living creatures full of eyes before and behind.
The first living creature like lion, the second like calf/young ox, the third having face like human, the fourth like flying eagle.

And the four living creatures, each having six wings, are full of eyes around and within, and they have no rest day and night saying: Holy, holy, holy, Lord Divine Almighty, the One who was and who is and who is coming.
And whenever the living creatures give glory and honor and thanks to the One sitting upon throne, the One living into the ages of ages,
the twenty-four elders fall before the One sitting upon throne and worship the One living into the ages of ages, and cast their crowns before the throne, saying:
Worthy are you, our Lord and Divine, to receive glory and honor and power, because you created all things, and through your will they were and were created.

translation_notes: Throne imagery draws from Ezekiel and Isaiah; symbols should remain symbolic.
Creation worship centers Divine worth before later judgment scenes.
Seven Spirits is apocalyptic fullness of Spirit language.
safety_note: Revelation 4 anchors the book in worship of Creator, not fear or spectacle.
